summary: Implementation bundles tests and code in a single feat: commit, violating TDD RED/GREEN separation. The hardcoded email placeholder is a correctness issue masked by weak type-only assertions. Test coverage is otherwise solid for error paths and cleanup behavior.

suggestions.description: Weak assertion masks hardcoded placeholder email
suggestions.rationale: The test checks typeof email === string which passes for the hardcoded user@no-tickets.com in the implementation. This is not testing real behavior — it is checking that a string is a string. If the email were extracted from the token response (as it should be), the test should assert the actual expected value.
suggestions.suggestion: Assert the specific email value that the OAuth flow should produce, or if email extraction is not yet implemented, add a TODO comment and a more descriptive test name acknowledging the placeholder.

suggestions.description: Email is hardcoded to user@no-tickets.com — not derived from auth flow
suggestions.rationale: The email returned to the caller is fabricated, not obtained from the authentication response. This means every user who authenticates gets the same email. The SESSION_DURATION_MS constant on line 15 similarly fabricates an expiration client-side rather than receiving it from the server. Both are placeholder implementations that should be called out explicitly.
suggestions.suggestion: If the auth server callback does not yet provide email or expiration, document this as a known limitation. At minimum, the email field should not pretend to be real — consider returning undefined or throwing until the server provides it.

suggestions.rationale: Same issue as the email assertion. expect(typeof expiresAt).toBe(string) will pass for any string. The test should verify the expiration is approximately 7 days in the future, matching the SESSION_DURATION_MS constant.
suggestions.suggestion: Assert that the expiresAt value parses to a date approximately 7 days from now, e.g. using a tolerance window.

suggestions.description: No test for authUrl that already contains query parameters
suggestions.rationale: The implementation constructs the URL with string concatenation: authUrl + ?callback_port=PORT. If authUrl already contains a query string (e.g. https://auth.example.com/cli?mode=popup), this produces a malformed URL with two ? characters.
suggestions.suggestion: Either add a test covering authUrl with existing query params (and fix the implementation to use URL/URLSearchParams), or document that authUrl must not contain query parameters.

suggestions.description: Test name claims expiration check but implementation delegates to loadCredentials
suggestions.rationale: The test is named returns existing credentials when valid and not expired but resolveInitAuth does not check expiration — it trusts loadCredentials to return null for expired tokens. The test name implies behavior that lives in a different module.
suggestions.suggestion: Rename to returns existing credentials when loadCredentials returns a value or similar, to accurately reflect what this unit is actually doing.
